description: Current actionable recommendations. Generated each session based on signals, predictions, and market forecasts. Accuracy is tracked to build confidence over time.

market_regime.label: DE-ESCALATION_REAL
market_regime.key_risk: 2-week ceasefire is truce, not permanent deal. April 10 Islamabad talks could collapse. War premium could return if talks fail.
market_regime.regime_note: CEASEFIRE CONFIRMED: April 8 morning (5:30 AM IST), Trump agreed to 2-week suspension + Iran accepted. Hormuz safe passage protocol established. Nifty GAPPED UP +3.72% to 23,984.85. Brent crashed -14.11% to $93.85 (BELOW $95 threshold — invalided MRPL bearish thesis). This is BEST ceasefire outcome for India: 2-week war premium extension + energy cost relief + rupee stability. Regime: Risk-ON with extended geopolitical tension management. Next test: April 10 Islamabad talks.
market_regime.session_forecast_accuracy_note: April 8 forecast was WRONG (predicted DOWN -0.8%, actual UP +3.72%) due to underweighting deal scenario in geopolitical binary. Pattern FORECAST_PAT003 identified: markets price best-case outcomes sooner than consensus. Framework updated.

actions.instruction: Sell 85 shares (50% ICICI lot) at ₹278–285 on any morning strength before 10:30 AM

actions.instruction: ELEVATED RISK — Iran rejected ceasefire. Trump Tuesday 8pm ET deadline is LIVE. Brent at $110.05 (in 100–115 base case zone but rising). If Brent breaks >$115 today OR Trump announces strikes → SELL 30–50% immediately before 10:30 AM. Do NOT wait. Ceasefire deal is OFF the table for now.
actions.rationale_short: IndiGo is the most Iran-sensitive holding. ATF cost = 30–35% of expenses. Brent at $110 = margin-compressing. War premium returning. Tuesday deadline is the new binary risk event.
actions.confidence_basis: Iran rejected ceasefire (Apr 7 morning confirmation). Escalation > de-escalation probability. Brent $110 = already hurting margins. Tuesday deadline could spike crude to $120+.

signal_details.M001: Brent crude at $109 — ATF = ~jet fuel * 1.1x. Every $10 Brent rise = ~₹200–300 EPS impact for IndiGo

actions.pnl_impact_if_right: Scenario B (escalation) → INDIGO -5 to -8% from current ₹4,271
actions.pnl_impact_if_wrong: Scenario C (deal) → INDIGO +6 to +10%. Missing this upside if sold too early.
actions.risk: Binary — wrong scenario call = significant miss

drill_down.why_now: Iran REJECTED ceasefire Apr 6-7. Trump Tuesday 8pm ET deadline is the new binary. IndiGo at ₹4,271 has major downside if Brent spikes to $120+ on escalation.
drill_down.what_could_go_wrong: Iran surprise reversal and accepts ceasefire → crude crashes → INDIGO gaps up. Very low probability given Iran's hard stance.
drill_down.watch_before_acting: FIRST action at 9:00 AM: check Brent. If >$112 and no deal news → lean toward partial sell. Tuesday 8pm ET is hard deadline — decide before then.

market_actions.instruction: Brent at $110.05 as of Apr 7 morning. Iran rejected ceasefire. Trump Tuesday 8pm ET deadline. Watch Brent through the day — escalation scenario is back.

market_actions.instruction: Nifty opened at 22,838 (Apr 7). Key resistance: 22,968 (Apr 6 close). Key support: 22,500 then 22,200. Iran war premium returning — P003 thesis reviving.

scenarios.below_22500: P003 accelerating — reduce all equity exposure. Move toward 22,200 support zone.
scenarios.below_22200: P003 TARGET ZONE — thesis confirming. Consider adding hedges.
